texto: I couldn't believe it.Something wasn't right.I thought, "Batman smokes cigarettes."I couldn't believe it. "Batman smokes fucking cigarettes."I walked away and saw that Batman was just this stupid guy dressed up in a rubber suit, just as afraid as I was, and that I lived in a lost place inside my own heart, where even Batman couldn't help me.
explicacao: Citação de Scott McClanahan sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “I couldn't believe it.Something wasn't right.I thought, "Batman smokes cigarettes."I couldn't bel…” articula amor, coming-of-age, crapalachia em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como luto e crapalachia.

frase_original: I couldn't believe it.Something wasn't right.I thought, "Batman smokes cigarettes."I couldn't believe it. "Batman smokes fucking cigarettes."I walked away and saw that Batman was just this stupid guy dressed up in a rubber suit, just as afraid as I was, and that I lived in a lost place inside my own heart, where even Batman couldn't help me.

texto: That night I dreamed about flying turtles and forest fires and fucking the earth...The next morning I awoke and I listened to the tree company tearing away the woods and the timber. I heard the chainsaws ripping outside my open window and I heard the dynamite exploding all the mountain tops away for the black rock below. And instead of feeling sad like I did most mornings, I felt something else now. I found myself saying, 'Explode. Explode you mountains. Rip them down you fuckers. Take this stinking dirt and leave this land with hatred and death.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Scott McClanahan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “That night I dreamed about flying turtles and forest fires and fucking the earth...The next morni…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, adolescence, coming-of-age pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e boa-noite.

frase_original: That night I dreamed about flying turtles and forest fires and fucking the earth...The next morning I awoke and I listened to the tree company tearing away the woods and the timber. I heard the chainsaws ripping outside my open window and I heard the dynamite exploding all the mountain tops away for the black rock below. And instead of feeling sad like I did most mornings, I felt something else now. I found myself saying, 'Explode. Explode you mountains. Rip them down you fuckers. Take this stinking dirt and leave this land with hatred and death.

texto: I never look at a painting and ask, "Is this painting fictional or non-fictional?" It's just a painting.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Scott McClanahan que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “I never look at a painting and ask, "Is this painting fictional or non-fictional?" It's just a pa…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, art, fiction, com foco em fiction e fictional.
